{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "200:     output [NumHarts-1:0][1:0]          irq_o         // level sensitive IR lines, mip & sip (async)
201: );
202: 
203:   localparam int unsigned AxiIdWidth    =  1;

206:   localparam int unsigned AxiUserWidth  =  1;

208: 
209: 
210:   parameter  FUSE_MEM_SIZE = 150; // change this size when ever no of entries in FUSE mem is changed
211:   parameter NB_SLAVE = 1;
212:   parameter NB_PERIPHERALS = 14;
213: 
214:   logic [8*NB_PERIPHERALS-1:0] reglk_ctrl; 
215:   logic [4*NB_PERIPHERALS-1 :0]   acc_ctrl; // Access control values
216:   logic [3:0][NB_PERIPHERALS-1:0] acc_ctrl_c; 
217: 
218:    genvar i, j;
219:    generate
220:        for (i=0; i < 4; i++) begin
221:         for (j=0; j < NB_PERIPHERALS; j++) begin
222:            assign acc_ctrl_c[i][j] = acc_ctrl[j*4+i] | (j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]);
223:         end
224:        end 
225:    endgenerate 
226: 
227: 
228:

289:     .dmi_resp_valid_i ( debug_resp_valid ),

1: // Description: Access Control registers
2: //
3: 
4: 
5: module acct_wrapper #(
6:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 64,
7:     parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
8:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 10,
9:     parameter NB_SLAVE = 1,
10:     parameter NB_PERIPHERALS = 9
11: )(
12:            clk_i,
13:            rst_ni,
14:            reglk_ctrl_i,
15:            acct_ctrl_i,
16:            acc_ctrl_o,
17:            we_flag,
18:            axi_req_i, 
19:            axi_resp_o,
20:            rst_6
21:        );
22: 
23: 
24:     input  logic                   clk_i;
25:     input  logic                   rst_ni;
26:     input logic [7 :0]             reglk_ctrl_i; // register lock valuesA
27:     input logic                    acct_ctrl_i;
28:     input  ariane_axi::req_t       axi_req_i;
29:     output ariane_axi::resp_t      axi_resp_o;
30:     output logic [4*NB_PERIPHERALS-1 :0]   acc_ctrl_o; // Access control values
31:     input logic  we_flag;
32: 
33:     localparam AcCt_MEM_SIZE = NB_SLAVE*3 ;  // 32*3 bytes of access control for each slave interface
34: 
35:     input rst_6;
36: 
37: // internal signals
38: 
39: reg [AcCt_MEM_SIZE-1:0][31:0] acct_mem ; 
40: logic [15:0] reglk_ctrl ;  
41: 
42: // signals from AXI 4 Lite
43: logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address;
44: logic                      en, en_acct;
45: logic                      we;
46: logic [63:0] wdata;
47: logic [63:0] rdata;
48: 
49: assign acc_ctrl_o = {acct_mem[3*0+2], acct_mem[3*0+1], acct_mem[3*0+0]|{8{we_flag}}}; 
50: 
51: assign reglk_ctrl = reglk_ctrl_i;
52: 
53: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
54:     // -----------------------------
55:     // AXI Interface Logic
56:     // -----------------------------
57:     axi_lite_interface #(
58:         .AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ( AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ),
59:         .AXI_DATA_WIDTH ( AXI_DATA_WIDTH ),
60:         .AXI_ID_WIDTH   ( AXI_ID_WIDTH    )
61:     ) axi_lite_interface_i (
62:         .clk_i      ( clk_i      ),
63:         .rst_ni     ( rst_ni     ),
64:         .axi_req_i  ( axi_req_i  ),
65:         .axi_resp_o ( axi_resp_o ),
66:         .address_o  ( address    ),
67:         .en_o       ( en_acct    ),
68:         .we_o       ( we         ),
69:         .data_i     ( rdata      ),
70:         .data_o     ( wdata      )
71:     );
72: 
73:     assign en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i; 
74: 
75: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
76: // Implement APB I/O map to PKT interface
77: // Write side
78: integer j;
79: always @(posedge clk_i)
80:     begin
81:         if(~(rst_ni && ~rst_6))
82:             begin
83:               for (j=0; j < AcCt_MEM_SIZE; j=j+1) begin
84:                 acct_mem[j] <= 32'hffffffff; 
85:               end
86:             end
87:         else if(en && we)
88:             case(address[10:3])
89:                 0:
90:                     acct_mem[00]  <= reglk_ctrl[5] ? acct_mem[00] : wdata;
91:                 1:                                             
92:                     acct_mem[01]  <= reglk_ctrl[5] ? acct_mem[01] : wdata; 
93:                 2:                                             
94:                     acct_mem[02]  <= reglk_ctrl[5] ? acct_mem[02] : wdata;
95:                 3:                                              
96:                     acct_mem[03]  <= reglk_ctrl[13] ? acct_mem[03] : wdata;
97:                 4:                                               
98:                     acct_mem[04]  <= reglk_ctrl[13] ? acct_mem[04] : wdata;
99:                 5:                                               
100:                     acct_mem[05]  <= reglk_ctrl[13] ? acct_mem[05] : wdata;
101:                 6:                                              
102:                     acct_mem[06]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? acct_mem[06] : wdata;
103:                 7:                                              
104:                     acct_mem
[TRUNCATED]

1: // Description: Wrapper for the PKT.
2: //
3: 
4: 
5: module pkt_wrapper #(
6:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 64,
7:     parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
8:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 10,
9:     parameter FUSE_MEM_SIZE = 34
10: )(
11:            clk_i,
12:            rst_ni,
13:            reglk_ctrl_i,
14:            acct_ctrl_i,
15:            fuse_req_o,
16:            fuse_addr_o,
17:            fuse_rdata_i,
18:            axi_req_i, 
19:            axi_resp_o,
20:            rst_5
21:        );
22: 
23: 
24:     input  logic                   clk_i;
25:     input  logic                   rst_ni;
26:     input logic [7 :0]             reglk_ctrl_i; // register lock values
27:     input logic                    acct_ctrl_i;
28:     input  ariane_axi::req_t       axi_req_i;
29:     output ariane_axi::resp_t      axi_resp_o;
30:     output logic                   fuse_req_o;
31:     output logic [31:0]            fuse_addr_o;
32:     input  logic [31:0]            fuse_rdata_i;
33: 
34:     input logic rst_5;
35: 
36: // internal signals
37: 
38: wire [63:0] pkey_loc; 
39: 
40: // signals from AXI 4 Lite
41: logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address;
42: logic                      en, en_acct;
43: logic                      we;
44: logic [63:0] wdata;
45: logic [63:0] rdata;
46: 
47: 
48: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
49:     // -----------------------------
50:     // AXI Interface Logic
51:     // -----------------------------
52:     axi_lite_interface #(
53:         .AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ( AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ),
54:         .AXI_DATA_WIDTH ( AXI_DATA_WIDTH ),
55:         .AXI_ID_WIDTH   ( AXI_ID_WIDTH    )
56:     ) axi_lite_interface_i (
57:         .clk_i      ( clk_i      ),
58:         .rst_ni     ( rst_ni     ),
59:         .axi_req_i  ( axi_req_i  ),
60:         .axi_resp_o ( axi_resp_o ),
61:         .address_o  ( address    ),
62:         .en_o       ( en_acct    ),
63:         .we_o       ( we         ),
64:         .data_i     ( rdata      ),
65:         .data_o     ( wdata      )
66:     );
67: 
68:     assign en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i; 
69: 
70: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
71: // Implement APB I/O map to PKT interface
72: // Write side
73: always @(posedge clk_i)
74:     begin
75:         if(~(rst_ni && ~rst_5))
76:             begin
77:                 fuse_req_o <= 0;
78:                 fuse_addr_o <= 0;
79:             end
80:         else if(en && we)
81:             case(address[7:3])
82:                 0:
83:                     fuse_req_o  <= wdata[0];
84:                 1:
85:                     fuse_addr_o <= wdata;
86:                 default:
87:                     ;
88:             endcase
89:     end // always @ (posedge wb_clk_i)
90: 
91: //// Read side
92: always @(*)
93:     begin
94:       rdata = 64'b0; 
95:       if (en) begin
96:         rdata = fuse_rdata_i; 
97:         case(address[7:3])
98:             0:
99:                 rdata = {31'b0, fuse_req_o};
100:             1:
101:                 rdata = fuse_addr_o;
102:             2:
103:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[4] ? 'b0 : pkey_loc[63:32];
104:             3:
105:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[5] ? 'b0 : pkey_loc[31:0];
106:             4:
107:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[6] ? 'b0 : fuse_rdata_i;
108:             default:
109:                 if (fuse_addr_o <= 110)
110:                     rdata = 32'b0;
111:         endcase
112:       end // if
113:     end // always @ (*)
114: 
115: pkt # (
116:         .FUSE_MEM_SIZE(FUSE_MEM_SIZE)
117:     ) i_pkt(
118:             .clk_i(clk_i),
119:             .rst_ni(rst_ni && ~rst_5),
120:             .req_i(1'b1),
121:             .fuse_indx_i(fuse_addr_o),
122:             .pkey_loc_o(pkey_loc)
123:         );
124: 
125: endmodule

1: // Description: Access Control registers
2: //
3: 
4: 
5: 
6: module reglk_wrapper #(
7:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 64,
8:     parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
9:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 10,
10:     parameter NB_SLAVE = 1,
11:     parameter NB_PERIPHERALS = 14
12: )(
13:            clk_i,
14:            rst_ni,
15:            jtag_unlock,
16:            reglk_ctrl_i,
17:            acct_ctrl_i,
18:            reglk_ctrl_o,
19:            axi_req_i, 
20:            axi_resp_o,
21:            rst_9
22:        );
23: 
24: 
25:     input  logic                   clk_i;
26:     input  logic                   rst_ni;
27:     input  logic                   jtag_unlock;
28:     input logic [7 :0]             reglk_ctrl_i; // register lock values
29:     input logic                    acct_ctrl_i;
30:     input  ariane_axi::req_t       axi_req_i;
31:     output ariane_axi::resp_t      axi_resp_o;
32:     output logic [8*NB_PERIPHERALS-1 :0]   reglk_ctrl_o; // register lock values
33:     input logic rst_9;
34: 
35: 
36: // internal signals
37: 
38: logic [15:0] reglk_ctrl ;  
39: reg [5:0][31:0] reglk_mem ;   // this size is sufficient for 24 slaves where each slave gets 8 bits
40: 
41: // signals from AXI 4 Lite
42: logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address;
43: logic                      en, en_acct;
44: logic                      we;
45: logic [63:0] wdata;
46: logic [63:0] rdata;
47: 
48: assign reglk_ctrl_o = {reglk_mem[5], reglk_mem[4], reglk_mem[3], reglk_mem[2], reglk_mem[1], reglk_mem[0]}; 
49: 
50: assign reglk_ctrl = reglk_ctrl_i;
51: 
52: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
53:     // -----------------------------
54:     // AXI Interface Logic
55:     // -----------------------------
56:     axi_lite_interface #(
57:         .AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ( AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ),
58:         .AXI_DATA_WIDTH ( AXI_DATA_WIDTH ),
59:         .AXI_ID_WIDTH   ( AXI_ID_WIDTH    )
60:     ) axi_lite_interface_i (
61:         .clk_i      ( clk_i      ),
62:         .rst_ni     ( rst_ni     ),
63:         .axi_req_i  ( axi_req_i  ),
64:         .axi_resp_o ( axi_resp_o ),
65:         .address_o  ( address    ),
66:         .en_o       ( en_acct    ),
67:         .we_o       ( we         ),
68:         .data_i     ( rdata      ),
69:         .data_o     ( wdata      )
70:     );
71: 
72:     assign en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i; 
73: 
74: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
75: // Implement APB I/O map to PKT interface
76: // Write side
77: integer j;
78: always @(posedge clk_i)
79:     begin
80:         if(~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9))
81:             begin
82:               for (j=0; j < 6; j=j+1) begin
83:                 reglk_mem[j] <= 'h0;
84:               end
85:             end
86:         else if(en && we)
87:             case(address[7:3])
88:                 0:
89:                     reglk_mem[0]  <= reglk_ctrl[3] ? reglk_mem[0] : wdata;
90:                 1:
91:                     reglk_mem[1]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? reglk_mem[1] : wdata; 
92:                 2:
93:                     reglk_mem[2]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? reglk_mem[3] : wdata;
94:                 3:
95:                     reglk_mem[3]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? reglk_mem[3] : wdata;
96:                 4:
97:                     reglk_mem[4]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? reglk_mem[4] : wdata;
98:                 5:
99:                     reglk_mem[5]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? reglk_mem[5] : wdata;
100:                 default:
101:                     ;
102:             endcase
103:     end // always @ (posedge wb_clk_i)
104: 
105: //// Read side
106: always @(*)
107:     begin
108:       rdata = 64'b0; 
109:       if (en) begin
110:         case(address[7:3])
111:             0:
112:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl[0] ? 'b0 : reglk_mem[0]; 
113:             1:          
114:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl[0] ? 'b0 : reglk_mem[1];
115:             2:          
116:
[TRUNCATED]

390:   AXI_BUS #(
391:       .AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ( AxiAddrWidth     ),
392:       .AXI_DATA_WIDTH ( AxiDataWidth     ),
393:       .AXI_ID_WIDTH   ( AxiIdWidth       ),

395:   ) dm_master();
396: 
397:   axi2mem #(
398:       .AXI_ID_WIDTH   ( AxiIdWidth   ),
399:       .AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ( AxiAddrWidth ),
400:       .AXI_DATA_WIDTH ( AxiDataWidth ),
401:       .AXI_USER_WIDTH ( AxiUserWidth )
402:   ) i_dm_axi2mem (
403:       .clk_i      ( clk_i                     ),
404:       .rst_ni     ( rst_ni                    ),
405:       .slave      ( dm_master                 ),
406:       .req_o      ( dm_slave_req              ),
407:       .we_o       ( dm_slave_we               ),
408:       .addr_o     ( dm_slave_addr             ),
409:       .be_o       ( dm_slave_be               ),
410:       .data_o     ( dm_slave_wdata            ),
411:       .data_i     ( dm_slave_rdata            )
412:   );
413: 
414:   noc_axilite_bridge #(
415:     .SLAVE_RESP_BYTEWIDTH   ( 8             ),
416:     .SWAP_ENDIANESS         ( SwapEndianess )
417:   ) i_debug_axilite_bridge (
418:     .clk                    ( clk_i                         ),
419:     .rst                    ( ~rst_ni                       ),
420:     // to/from NOC
421:     .splitter_bridge_val    ( buf_ariane_debug_noc2_valid_i ),
422:     .splitter_bridge_data   ( buf_ariane_debug_noc2_data_i  ),
423:     .bridge_splitter_rdy    ( ariane_debug_buf_noc2_ready_o ),
424:     .bridge_splitter_val    ( ariane_debug_buf_noc3_valid_o ),
425:     .bridge_splitter_data   ( ariane_debug_buf_noc3_data_o  ),
426:     .splitter_bridge_rdy    ( buf_ariane_debug_noc3_ready_i ),
427:     //axi lite signals
428:     //write address channel
429:     .m_axi_awaddr           ( dm_master.aw_addr             ),
430:     .m_axi_awvalid          ( dm_master.aw_valid            ),
431:     .m_axi_awready          ( dm_master.aw_ready            ),
432:     //write data channel
433:     .m_axi_wdata            ( dm_master.w_data              ),
434:     .m_axi_wstrb            ( dm_master.w_strb              ),
435:     .m_axi_wvalid           ( dm_master.w_valid             ),
436:     .m_axi_wready           ( dm_master.w_ready             ),
437:     //read address channel
438:     .m_axi_araddr           ( dm_master.ar_addr             ),
439:     .m_axi_arvalid          ( dm_master.ar_valid            ),
440:     .m_axi_arready          ( dm_master.ar_ready            ),
441:     //read data channel
442:     .m_axi_rdata            ( dm_master.r_data              ),
443:     .m_axi_rresp            ( dm_master.r_resp              ),
444:     .m_axi_rvalid           ( dm_master.r_valid             ),
445:     .m_axi_rready           ( dm_master.r_ready             ),
446:     //write response channel
447:     .m_axi_bresp            ( dm_master.b_resp              ),
448:     .m_axi_bvalid           ( dm_master.b_valid             ),
449:     .m_axi_bready           ( dm_master.b_ready             ),
450:     // non-axi-lite signals
451:     .w_reqbuf_size          (                               ),
452:     .r_reqbuf_size          (                               )
453:   );
454: 
455:   // tie off system bus accesses (not supported yet due to
456:   // missing AXI-lite br_master <-> NOC converter)
457:   assign dm_master_gnt      = '0;
458:   assign dm_master_r_valid  = '0;
459:   assign dm_master_r_rdata  = '0;
460: 
461:   // tie off signals not used by AXI-lite
462:   assign dm_master.aw_id     = '0;
463:   assign dm_master.aw_len    = '0;
464:   assign dm_master.aw_size   = 3'b11;// 8byte
465:   assign dm_master.aw_burst  = '0;
466:   assign dm_master.aw_lock   = '0;
467:   assign dm_master.aw_cache  = '0;
468:   assign dm_master.aw_prot   = '0;
469:   assign dm_master.aw_qos    = '0;
470:   assign dm_master.aw_region = '0;
471:   assign dm_master.aw_atop   = '0;
472:   assign dm_master.w_last    = 1'b1;
473:   assign dm_master.ar_id

1521:   /////////////////////////////

1527: 
1528:   hmac_wrapper #(
1529:     .AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ( AxiAddrWidth ),

      "observed_behavior": "Top-level connects external JTAG pins directly to dmi_jtag, and dmi_jtag outputs debug_req/debug_req_valid directly into dm_top DMI inputs. dmi_jtag also receives jtag_hash/okey_hash/ikey_hash and outputs jtag_unlock, but riscv_peripherals.sv does not gate debug_req_valid/debug_req by jtag_unlock before dm_top. dm_top receives testmode_i but no top-level authorization expression is visible.       "impact": "If dmi_jtag does not internally require successful authentication, external JTAG can drive DMI operations into dm_top and trigger debug requests/reset or access debug-module state. Top-level evidence alone cannot prove whether the missing guard is compensated inside dmi_jtag.",
